code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment based solely on the review text
    - Base rating on holistic satisfaction, not isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards/multi-purchase failures overriding positives
    - Fundamental usability failures despite concept appeal
    - Premature failure (≤6 months) with functional consequences
    - "Avoid purchase" warnings with defect evidence
    - Negative updates erase previous satisfaction

    2★ (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Partial functionality with persistent frustration
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Regret purchase" statements with justification

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Functional limitation in primary intended use case
    - Requires preprocessing/break-in for full function
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only 4 because...")
    - Brand comparisons with minor shortcomings
    - Meeting expectations without enthusiastic endorsement

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Solves persistent problems through daily reliability
    - 3+ practical benefits praised (durability/utility/cost-effectiveness)
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" despite imperfections
    - Targeted recommendations solving specific group needs
    - Replacement purchases indicating satisfaction
    - Concise enthusiastic praise ("Perfect!", "Love it!")

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - Continuous part replacements + core satisfaction →5
    - "Works as intended" + validation →5 / neutral confirmation →4
    - Primary use limitation →4 even if suitable for alternatives
    - Brief praise + functional flaw →4 / + enthusiastic →5
    - Multi-aspect criticism + final endorsement →5
    - Cost-effective solutions for specific needs →5
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Best for first kitchens despite experienced cooks' limitations"
<Reasoning>Targeted recommendation solving specific needs →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "This mug is beautiful but too small for coffee. Perfect for tea though!"
<Reasoning>Primary use limitation despite alternative suitability →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Stasher bag great but don't overstuff seal"
<Reasoning>Usage caveat limits functionality →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Perfect magnets! Strong hold and ideal size"
<Reasoning>Enthusiastic concise praise →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Now I can go magnet crazy. Right size and strength"
<Reasoning>Brief praise without enthusiasm →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Good for light use. Thin material concerns for heavy loads"
<Reasoning>Adequate with notable compromise →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Weekly adjustments needed for basic function"
<Reasoning>Persistent maintenance →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Pyrex containers remain essential despite replacing lids multiple times"
<Reasoning>Continuous core satisfaction through replacements →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "UPDATE: Failed after 3mo. Never again this brand"
<Reasoning>Definitive failure update →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (30 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 11
  gold 5 -> 4: 9
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 1 -> 4: 1
  gold 3 -> 2: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I like my Star Wars characters. I have a Darth Vader mug already and now I have the Chewbacca. The cup is nice and I like that it has the sash detail on the front and back so you know immediately it is Chewie. The mug can hold 20 ounces. As with most..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "On my computer screen the color for the 'cool brown' bath mat is accurate.  It's a faded sort of brown with lots of gray tint to it.  Our upstairs bath is in browns -- golden-hue brown and tile brown-- and it fits in.

What you get is a cool-brown no..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "This bathroom rug is so plush and soft. One of my dogs tried to claim it while I had it laid out on the couch. It is 100% organic cotton that is sustainable. So you help the environment and the natural resources. This rug is machine washable, gentle ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I think a toaster should last longer than 2 years.
We purchased this toaster in April 2016.  Last month a knob broke off.  Yesterday the right slot stopped working.

I don't know why it's impossible to make a good toaster anymore but this one makes b..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This mattress get 4 stars for comfort, but there's so many other nice features about it that it gets 5 starts for everything else. To begin with, I wouldn't suggest using this as a long term every day bed for someone. The shallow design and budget pr..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "It's a very solid bed that feels like it's going to hold up to just about anything. Assembly wasn't too difficult, though there were a couple of parts that had to be under some pressure to get the screw holes to line up just right. Also, when we firs..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 30 errors, 21 borderline out of 80 examples."}
